comment: Authored cutscenes that re-create EarthBound scripted scenes the converter drops (NPC choreography: walk out a door, line up, appear/vanish). Run scripts/scan-broken-scenes.mjs for the inventory; use. Scene <ccsRef> for a scene's NPC ids + door coords.

cutscenes.comment: Police station (data_15 cop row, npcs 73-77 sprite group 74 @ y=192). EB had them walk off via raw [1F F1] codes + {set(flag 289)}. They turn, lose interest, file out the right door (7640,208) one by one, and stay gone (EB flag 289).

pages: The officers size you up, then lose interest all at once.
"Shift's over. Whatever this is, it's the next guy's problem."

cutscenes.comment: data_14.l_0xc5e840, near world pixel 2728,608 (cluster of NPCs 150/189/190/199 at ~2752,544, just outside the Giant Step cave doors). EB choreography: pause(60), hide_char_float(5), window_open(1), dialogue, char_direction(1,0) = the lead companion (NPC 150, sprite group 74) turns to face the player (down) and nags them about wandering instead of getting the kid home. The trailing companion 'floats off' (hide_char_float) and is hidden afterward. Voiced Swagbound noir; player watches then is released. | GATED OFF post-intro-rework (blockFlags intro:bedroom-opening-done): the escort-home framing is stale for the solo wake-up opening, and its moveActor on gawker 199 deadlocks once flag 469 despawns the crowd (invisible-actor cutscene class). Re-frame or retire in the Phase-2 dialogue pass.

pages: A hand lands on your shoulder before you reach the dark.
"Hey. So that's the plan? Just drift wherever the wind takes you?"
"There's a kid counting on us to get him home. The wandering can wait."

cutscenes.comment: data_14 / placement @ worldPixel 2008,112, eventFlag 466, NPC 124 (sprite group 382). EB choreography markers: hide_char, show_char, party_add. EB beat (data_14.ccs:313-324) is the big-brother 'are you just gonna wander around wherever you please?' lecture that ends in {unset(flag 466)} and a party/flag transition. Re-creates it as: the companion (NPC 124) materializes (show_char), faces the player, says his dry piece, then falls in and vanishes from the field (party_add -> hide_char). Gated on EB flag 466; clears it on completion so it never re-fires. Bystanders 165/193/198 are left alone.

pages: He steps out of the dark like he's been waiting for you to notice.
"So. You're just gonna wander wherever you please. No plan, no backup, no clue."
"...Sometimes I wonder which one of us is supposed to be the responsible one."
He sighs the sigh of a man who already knows the answer. "Fine. I'm coming. Somebody's gotta watch your back."

cutscenes.comment: data_20.l_0xc66e40 -> l_0xc66eca (the 'Welcome home / go to bed' beat). The EB house occupant npcId 148 (sprite group 145) is at world pixel 2656,344 but is not visibly present in this Swagbound area-trigger staging; co-located npcId 183 (group 227) stays put. EB: mom greets the player, player picks Yes, music_effect(3) + a wall of flag sets (295/469/530/473/468/749, unset 474/11/517), hide_char(255), two warps into the bedroom, lock_movement(255), then show_party(1) for the morning. Re-staged as ambient house narration, then hide the occupant state (lights out), then setFlag the lights-out signal. lockPlayer true so the player watches; steps always resolve so the player is released.

pages: The door is unlocked. It's always unlocked. That's the kind of town this still is, for now.
Whatever's out there past the streetlights can wait til morning. It's not going anywhere. Neither is the house.
Lights go out. Nobody talks about the noise tonight.

cutscenes.comment: data_51.l_0xc999a1 (Act 1, Minch house door @ worldPixel 2392,344, eventFlag 0x1d4). NOTE: world npcId 182 (sprite group 227) is the yard MAILBOX, not the brother; the speaker is the added NPC 100101 (Pokey-analog kid, group 44 skin) placed by the door. EB choreography = char_direction(1,0): he turns to face the player (down) while reminding them to bring Picky back before dad gets home, then offers a little help via {set(flag 2)}. Reauthored as a one-shot area cutscene bracketing 2392,344; lockPlayer so the player watches the turn + line, steps always complete so the player is released.

pages: The kid by the door clocks you and doesn't bother getting up.
"Like I said. Dad's not home yet. Small mercy."
"You drag Picky back here before he walks through that door, or it's both our hides."
"...Fine. I'll throw you a bone. Don't make me regret it."

cutscenes.comment: ACT-1 MORNING: monologue removed (Nick 2026-07-19: no key story/guidance via narration). Now a silent flag-setter; the Swag Deck hand-off + how-to-use + wayfinding are delivered as a SCENE by Bosch's parent (trigger signal-town-parent-swag-deck) and shown household behavior.

data_56.l_0xef617b near world pixel 3280,5504. EB Belch-base gatekeeper npcId 688 asks for Fly Honey, rejects the answer, calls the close-window movement helper, then raw [1F F1 B0 02 7B 02] sends the guard through movement 0x027b and sets flag 366. Recast as a Postwick toll clerk checking whether Bosch brought a sweet enough record.

pages: A toll clerk steps out with a smile that smells like sugar and inventory glue.
"Delivery boy, claimant, guest. Same floor, different donation."
"If you brought something sweet, the group owns the sweetness. If you didn't, the group wants to know why."

cutscenes.comment: data_56.l_0xef62f5 near world pixel 2192,5744. EB guard npcId 691 demands the Fly Honey password, decides silence is suspicious, calls data_21.l_0xc68546, then raw [1F F1 B3 02 7B 02] clears the guard and sets flag 367. Recast as a Postwick password audit on the arena approach.

pages: The clerk's clipboard already has a blank where Bosch's password should be.
"Say the word that proves you were invited."
Bosch says nothing useful. The blank decides that is evidence.
"Quiet people are either shy or dangerous. Postwick bills both at the same rate."

cutscenes.comment: data_56.l_0xef63b5 near world pixel 2800,5504. EB guard npcId 692 recognizes the party as the ones who destroyed assigned Threed zombies, calls data_21.l_0xc6854d, then raw [1F F1 B4 02 7B 02] retires the guard and sets flag 368. Recast as a Postwick foreman losing control of the assigned crowd.

pages: "You're the one who spoiled my assigned dead."
The foreman's pen shakes. Not from grief. From paperwork.
"I had them sorted by face, appetite, and acceptable stink. Then you made them individuals again."
"That is not liberation. That is inventory damage."

cutscenes.comment: data_60.l_0xef909e near world pixel 3184,6168. EB photo-marker npcId 1169 runs the standard photo packet: [1F 23 BB 01] materializes the photographer cue, then raw [1F F1 91 04 7B 02] dismisses actor 0x0491 and sets flag 393. Recast as a Postwick crowd camera trying to certify Bosch's public mood.

pages: A camera drops into the street like it had an appointment with Bosch's face.
"Smile like you're in on the bit."
The flash catches the crowd first, Bosch second, and the one anon who still does not get the joke.

cutscenes.comment: data_60.l_0xef924e near world pixel 3152,6168. EB photo-marker npcId 1170 uses [1F 23 BC 01], then raw [1F F1 92 04 7B 02] moves/dismisses actor 0x0492 and sets flag 407. Recast as the second Postwick camera in the same crowd line.

pages: Another lens rises before the first flash has cooled.
"Retake. The last one made you look like you might leave."
Bosch keeps that look. The lens wants everyone on-message.

cutscenes.comment: data_60.l_0xef926d near world pixel 3464,5944. EB photo-marker npcId 1171 runs [1F 23 BC 01], then raw [1F F1 93 04 7B 02] clears actor 0x0493 and sets flag 408. Recast as a side-door photo check near the arena route.

pages: The side door coughs up a photographer with union posture and borrowed joy.
"Postwick needs proof you had fun at onboarding."
"Stand still. Put one on. The picture looks better when nobody asks what it cost."

cutscenes.comment: data_60.l_0xef9502 near world pixel 4048,5672. EB Master Belch/Puke encounter npcId 590 calls data_56.l_0xef681a, resolves a battle helper, calls data_56.l_0xef68d7, then raw [1F F1 4E 02 7B 02] clears actor 0x024e and sets flag 603. Recast as Postwick's complaint engine measuring Bosch as a commoner.

pages: A sour little official waddles in with a mouth full of policy.
"Commoner. You wander without Honey, without pass, without a Milady to show the floor."
"Master Belch loved a clean appetite. Postwick loves a recruited crowd. You are neither."

cutscenes.comment: data_24.l_0xc6d50a near world pixel 4504,5864. EB backstage guard npcId 840 blocks the restricted door until the Runaway Five condition, then raw [1F F1 48 03 F2 00], [1F 1E 48 03 06], and [1F 17 49 03 08 00 01] walk the guard aside, adjust the door actor, and set flag 374 plus a branch flag. Recast as a Postwick route attendant letting Bosch through the performed crowd.

pages: The attendant blocks the service door with a smile trained to deny everything softly.
"Backstage is for people in the bit. Frontstage is where we do the onboarding."
He reads Bosch's damaged card, frowns at the bad print, and steps aside anyway.
"Fine. The crowd paid to see somebody refuse the mask. Try not to enjoy it."

cutscenes.comment: data_60.l_0xef92ca near world pixel 3696,8504. EB photo-marker npcId 1179 runs [1F 23 BC 01], then raw [1F F1 9B 04 7B 02] dismisses actor 0x049b and sets flag 411. Recast as a Dead Letter street camera recording Bosch as already returned mail.

pages: Dead Letter's first camera doesn't flash. It receipts.
"Returned before delivery. Face intact enough for routing."
Bosch does not sign. The receipt prints anyway.

cutscenes.comment: data_60.l_0xef9118 near world pixel 4400,9656. EB photo-marker npcId 1176 uses [1F 23 BB 01], then raw [1F F1 98 04 7B 02] clears actor 0x0498 and sets flag 397. Recast as a return-line camera near the undelivered route.

pages: A return-line camera leans out of the dark and finds Bosch without hunting.
"Hold still. Undelivered things blur when they remember who sent them."
The lens catches the blur. The town calls that accuracy.

cutscenes.comment: data_60.l_0xef92ab near world pixel 4432,9656. EB photo-marker npcId 1177 uses [1F 23 BC 01], then raw [1F F1 99 04 7B 02] clears actor 0x0499 and sets flag 410. Recast as the paired undelivered-photo beat beside the return line.

pages: The second camera is already tired of Bosch's first answer.
"Retake. This town prefers its dead mail legible."
"Legible means owned by the reader. You understand."

cutscenes.comment: data_60.l_0xef9448 near world pixel 4296,8944. EB photo-marker npcId 1180 runs [1F 23 D4 01], then raw [1F F1 9C 04 7B 02] clears actor 0x049c and sets flag 435. Recast as a plate-camera on the road between Dead Letter's arrival and museum lanes.

pages: A camera plate clicks open like a little coffin lid.
"Proof of passage. Proof of personhood pending."
The plate takes Bosch's outline and leaves the inside deliberately blank.

cutscenes.comment: data_54.l_0xc9e619 near world pixel 5088,7608. EB Master Barf/Puke scene npcId 1229 taunts the party, plays sound 78, calls the battle helper, then party_add(4), learnpsi(4,2), sets flag 276, and raw [1F F1 CD 04 7B 02] clears actor 0x04cd. Recast as a museum-path return of a bad copy that insists it improved.

pages: Something sour returns wearing a better title.
"You forgot me because the cult put me in the bit. That is how rot becomes a brand."
"Call me Puke if you need the old joke. Call me onboarded if you want the floor to clap."
It bows like a citation and leaks off the path.

cutscenes.comment: data_25.l_0xc6fae5 plus textPointer2 data_25.l_0xc6fb02 near world pixel 6872,10024. EB first reports the locked/bent-key path, then the locker-key branch calls l_0xc6fb33, runs [1F 1E DF 01 06], and sets flag 541. Recast as the Dead Letter door refusing Bosch's key while a distant call tries to route him.

pages: The lock accepts the key, thinks about it, and returns a worse answer.
"Bent. Unverified. Not shaped for this claim."
From somewhere past the door, a voice calls Bosch by a name that almost fits.
"We are trying to contact the one the route cannot deliver."

cutscenes.comment: data_31.l_0xc795c1 near world pixel 7000,9808. EB rescue scene uses the key, thanks Ness, moves npcId 436 and companion markers with raw [1F F1 B5 01 F1 00] plus [1F 1E B6 01 06] and [1F 1E B5 01 06], party_add(2), then sets flags 13/198/321/689/483 and unsets 118. Recast as a Dead Letter echo of a rescued psychic choosing to walk back under her own name.

pages: A voice behind the dead mail slot answers before Bosch knocks.
"You are just like I imagined you would be. That is not a compliment. Imagination can be another cage."
"People are worried in the town that onboarded me. Good. Worry is proof I got out before they could own me."
"I can walk back if I walk as myself. Stand close. The route hates witnesses."

cutscenes.comment: Optional Correction restoration beat after the field agent is beaten. No PHOTO flags or Archivist mechanics are touched; this only clears the planted fake marker for a later records-view hook.

pages: The motel sign loses a letter, then finds it again.
Room 1's guest blinks. "No. Bosch refused onboarding. I remember refusing now."
Room 2's receipt uncurls. "The pledge did not get signed. It got torn up."
Room 3's brochure folds itself blank. "The museum did not get its convert. It lost the lie."
Room 4 hears the marquee cough. "Bosch noticed. That was the part the cult could not turn into a joke."
Room 5 closes the suitcase. "Morningside logged Bosch as not onboarded. Messy. Better."
ARCHIVIST: "Someone put the milady over my filing. I am filing what was underneath."

cutscenes.comment: Optional Floor counter beat for the sold path. No PHOTO mechanics; only files the side-content fragment.

pages: The kid returns with a button polished flat by one small thumb.
KID: "I want my drawing back. This button is all I have."
VENDOR: "Accepted. The market is whoever shows up."
The tag comes off the drawing. The drawing looks more expensive without it.
ARCHIVIST: "Value is a rumor. Worth is a witness. Filed under The Floor."

cutscenes.comment: Optional Floor counter beat for the refused path. No PHOTO mechanics; only files the side-content fragment.

pages: The kid returns with a button polished flat by one small thumb.
KID: "I want my drawing back. This button is all I have."
VENDOR: "Accepted. The market is whoever shows up."
The tag comes off the drawing. The drawing looks more expensive without it.
ARCHIVIST: "Value is a rumor. Worth is a witness. Filed under The Floor."

cutscenes.comment: Optional Onboarding pew session at Solana Beach. Placement keyed to source-pier trigger/interactable around (7464,2864)/(7496,2864), the existing chapel NPC cluster at (7472..7608,2504..2552), and solana-beach rect x3800..7800 y2500..3800. The follow-up trigger grants item 173 because cutscene steps cannot grant items.

pages: The chapel pew receives Bosch with the confidence of a sales funnel.
FACILITATOR: "Welcome to onboarding. Belief is a network effect. Doubt is churn with shoes."
Everyone repeats the pledge at the same speed. Bosch does not, and is still marked present.
The session ends when the room agrees it has always been over.

cutscenes.comment: Optional Onboarding counter at the chapel sound board, keyed to source-pier trigger/interactable around (7464,2864)/(7496,2864) and solana-beach rect x3800..7800 y2500..3800. Eight Sources content only grants item 196 tracks through trigger grantItems; actual playback of the kid's own first recording needs a runtime Mixtape/audio hook, so this cutscene expresses playback in dialogue only.

pages: At the chapel sound board, a kid has labeled every slider DOUBT and turned them all down.
KID: "I was going to leave. The floor called that bad for morale. Nice onboarding, apparently."
Bosch threads the kid's own first recording through the board. A small voice says he was scared before he was useful.
The kid listens to himself breathe without permission.
KID: "That's me. Not the me they put the milady on. Gross. Good."
The chapel loses one member and gains one honest silence.

cutscenes.comment: Optional Onboarding filing beat after the kid walks out. No PHOTO flags or Archivist mechanics are touched; this only files the side-content fragment. Placement flavor is keyed to the sound board near source-pier and the same solana-beach rect.

pages: The chapel ledger snaps shut without being touched.
ARCHIVIST: "A room filed a kid's doubt as noise. We filed the noise as a witness."
ARCHIVIST: "The Onboarding. Filed under networks that call hunger community."

cutscenes.comment: Optional Unsigned counter beat when the Surviving Signature returns to the Galleria service floor. Placement keyed to source-vault boss at (2440,6728), source-vault interactable at (2408,6728), and the-galleria rect x1900..6500 y3100..6900. No PHOTO flags or Archivist mechanics are touched; this sets fuel:unsigned:cleared for a later Records menu page hook and the authored shelf-mask prop beat.

pages: The Unsigned runner holds the receipt like it might bite him back into shape.
UNSIGNED RUNNER: "Tobin Vale. Ugly little syllables. Mine, unfortunately. Mine, thankfully."
The Archivist files the signature under a name and the name under a person.
ARCHIVIST: "A name is a debt other people agree to remember."
A record page opens where the blank used to sit. Tobin takes off the mask and leaves it on the shelf like a tool that failed upward.

cutscenes.comment: data_20.l_0xc65ea3 near world pixel 1512,1744. Anchor: signal-town-card-clique boss at the MONS LINK arcade corner (1512,1744). EB raw [1F F1 8E 00 7B 02] has Shark npcId 142 press the player, then sets flag 363. Recast as a pre-fight card-clique shakedown that folds before the real boss gate.

pages: A kid in a backward cap steps into Bosch's lane and fans three cards like a bad legal argument.
"You wanna start something, or do you just look like paperwork that learned to walk?"
His friend closes the side angle. Two faces, one routine, both waiting for Bosch to flinch.
Bosch does not. The routine loses interest in being brave.

cutscenes.comment: ACT-1 arcade approach. FULLY STAGED (2026-07-19): the 'screen shows the corrected Bosch' beat is now an actual staged moment - camera push-in on the kid (143), a whoosh + white flash as the screen lights up, then his taunt - instead of a narrated description. Exterior street, so camera focus/zoom holds.

pages: "You want to meet Frank? Everybody wants to meet Frank, right up until Frank puts the milady on them."
THE KID: "See? You're in the bit. Calmer. A little bit nicer. You look better already."
"Go on in. The masked kid they recruited to be you is waiting. He's been practicing the bit since MiFella's picture taught him how."

cutscenes.comment: data_20.l_0xc660a6 near world pixel 1616,1616. Anchor: signal-town-card-clique sets signal:clique_cleared at the arcade, then this post-win scene brackets Frank npcId 146 and the nearest GAME door at 1592,1680. EB has Fail-proof Frank concede, set several Onett flags, and raw [1F F1 CA 00 7B 02] walks him off. Recast as the clean Bosch copy losing its warranty.

pages: Frank stares at the dent in his perfect onboarding record like it might apologize first.
"No dropouts. That was the pitch. Turns out the pitch fails when the recruit keeps moving."
Behind him, Bosch's membership card bends at one corner. It hates being handled by weather.
"You want the route? Fine. The town keeps a door behind the show, and the mayor keeps acting like a key is a moral achievement."
"Go bother the desk with the hat. Tell them the recruit they paid for took off the mask."

cutscenes.comment: data_47.l_0xc94abc near world pixel 2224,6576. Anchor: Postwick Venue floor and source-vault path, between arena-venue brackets and the Floor side-content triggers around y=6400..6728. EB bus NPC 320 asks for a ride to Threed; the Yes branch raw [1F F1 75 01 B5 00] boards the bus. Recast as a Venue shuttle heckler offering Bosch a group ride to a pre-approved mood.

pages: A shuttle tout leans out from the Venue curb with a grin laminated against weather.
"Everybody aboard for Postwick joy. Loud bus, clean route, no private thoughts under the seats."
"You coming, Bosch? The group rate is cheaper if we remove the individual first."
Bosch lets the bus idle without him. It sounds relieved to have one less witness.

cutscenes.comment: data_54.l_0xc9ec7a near world pixel 5496,7880. Anchor: Dead Letter museum route after deadletter:arrived and before deadletter-act3-end; nearest museum door pair sits around 5248,8328. EB turns nearby actors with char_direction and reveals the machine is broken because it has no engine. Recast as a museum display admitting the delivery system was hollow.

pages: The display clicks, coughs, and presents a diagram of a delivery engine with the engine missing.
"Completely broken," says the attendant. "On re-examination, it appears there was never a motor."
Dead Letter calls this conservation. A thing cannot fail in transit if it never had the means to move.

cutscenes.comment: data_40.l_0xc8905c near world pixel 5000,10008. Anchor: source-undelivered yard at 5388,9692 and Dead Letter return-line route; hidden npcId 770 shares the scene with npcId 795. EB greets the lead by name, unsets flag 272, plays music, then raw [1F F1 1B 03 01 01] moves the caller. Recast as a returned call that knows Bosch before the route delivers him.

pages: A return-phone rings without a phone around it.
"Bosch. Greetings. Your address arrived after your absence, so we opened the absence first."
"Please stay on the line while we decide whether you were ever the sender."

cutscenes.comment: data_46.l_0xc91c3a near world pixel 6760,10032. Anchor: Dead Letter bent-key/return route around 6872,10024, near doors at 6864,10024 and 6664,9960. EB Sky Runner repair scene turns actor 3, hides it with hide_char_float, then the repair branch locks movement and runs five raw [1F F1] actor moves. Recast as a routing clerk admitting the problem cannot be seen from the back of the file.

pages: "I cannot see the problem from this vantage point."
The routing clerk steps around the file like the front might confess what the back was paid to hide.
"There. The address is not wrong. The town is reading it from behind."

cutscenes.comment: data_44.l_0xc9067d near world pixel 6744,9280. Anchor: Dead Letter hotel-like return route, after deadletter:arrived and before act3:complete; nearest doors at 6792,9272 and 6664,9320. EB Threed Sunset Hotel stay accepts payment, sets hotel/rest flags, then jumps into a long lock_movement and hide_char dream sequence. Recast as a Dead Letter clerk offering Bosch a room that sorts him while he sleeps.

pages: "Welcome to the Sunset Hotel. One night costs whatever cash the floor can talk you out of."
"We provide clean sheets, quiet halls, and dreams that join the group before you do."
Bosch declines the pillow. The clerk marks him PRESENT for morning gm anyway.

cutscenes.comment: data_53.l_0xc9c991 near world pixel 6824,8296. Anchor: post-museum route after act3:complete, with doors around 6864,8168 and 6992,8328. EB says to return to Mu, then reachable labels hide the party, warp, party_add three members, party_remove one, and lock movement. Recast as an empty-room echo asking Bosch to revisit the place that cannot be filed.

pages: "I will not say anything," says the empty room, which is a lot of confidence for a room.
"Go to Mu again. The place of emptiness still has your name on nothing."
Bosch hears a roster shuffle behind the silence. Nobody signs for it.

cutscenes.comment: data_54.l_0xc9e613 near world pixel 5496,7344. Anchor: museum approach after deadletter:arrived and before act3:complete; scanner reaches data_44.l_0xc90dba, which locks movement after the hotel checkout line. Recast as a bed receipt that thanks Bosch for resting in a room he refused.

pages: "Didn't you think it was comfortable?"
The clerk is pointing at a bed Bosch never used and a charge he apparently slept through.
"Take care and goodbye. Dead Letter values guests who leave before arriving."

cutscenes.comment: data_54.l_0xc9e37e near world pixel 6176,7144. Anchor: Dead Letter museum approach after deadletter:arrived and before act3:complete. EB monkey-teleport scene asks to learn teleport, then raw [1F F1 CA 04 D1 01] and follow-up actor ops stage failed and successful attempts. Recast as an undelivered clerk trying to route himself by momentum.

pages: "I heard a person can teleport if they run hard enough at certainty."
The clerk takes three serious steps, collides with the concept of delivery, and calls it a trial run.
"I will never give up. I want to fail from farther away next time."

cutscenes.comment: data_42.l_0xc8b52a near world pixel 7264,2928. Anchor: source-pier trigger at 7464,2864 inside the Solana Beach route. EB boatman offers a ride, then the accepted branch reaches data_46.l_0xc92c70 with lock_movement, hide_char, raw boat movement, Kraken flags 542/543, and a post-battle return. Recast as the pier offering Bosch broadcast transit before the source is cleared.

pages: "Boat ride? Twenty bucks per person. Group rate, if everybody puts one on."
The captain points at the water. "Luck determines the outcome. Luck and whoever the floor puts on the list."
Bosch stays on the pier. The captain keeps pitching the crossing to nobody.

cutscenes.comment: data_41.l_0xc8b191 near world pixel 5184,2864. Anchor: Solana Beach source-pier route after act3:complete; hidden npcId 1069 sits near doors at 5136,2888 and 5232,2904. EB Magic Cake scene unsets flag 61, hides the party, warps into the Mu dream, party_adds one member and removes the others, then locks movement. Recast as a broadcast-pier vendor selling a communal dream as onboarding.

pages: "I used every leftover ingredient. This is a very special cake."
The vendor smiles like a person who found a way to monetize a dream before the sleeper did.
"Eat it and you will finally get the joke. Everyone else already does."
Bosch leaves the slice untouched. It keeps trying to recruit him anyway.

cutscenes.comment: data_47.l_0xc936e6 near world pixel 136,400. Anchor: source-first-record trigger at 148,404 in The Unlisted Room. EB Tenda/Shy scene branches to data_47.l_0xc9370d, runs raw [1F F1 EF 04 54 02], plays sound 121, and sets flag 153 after the shy power breaks through. Recast as the first record finding the nerve to be heard.

pages: "Shy. That we are."
The record under the room's one light inhales like it has been waiting for someone else to start the chant.
"Powerful. I so show everyone."
It finds the nerve. Nobody says gm, and it keeps going anyway.

cutscenes.comment: data_15.l_0xc606d8 near world pixel 3160,9944. Anchor: source-vacancy trigger at 2720,9560 and the Vacancy Flats motel strip. EB broken slot machine asks for a buck; accepted branch data_15.l_0xc607e4 runs four raw [1F F1] moves for the Sanchez Brothers trio around npcIds 765..767. Recast as the motel machine trying to buy Bosch's cheer with his own money.

pages: The broken slot machine clears its throat in coins it does not have.
"Insert a buck and the cheerful trio will make you happy."
Three smiling attendants twitch in the reflection. They know the bit. Bosch does not.
Bosch keeps the buck. The machine calls that a failed donation.

cutscenes.comment: New opening meteor gathering. On arrival, establish the meteor sitting in the crater and the six Milady manifestations around it (each says only 'milady' when talked to). Then MiFella gives the go-home-to-bed beat. The witnesses and the go-uphill hint are gone.

pages: A meteor sits in the crater, still warm, humming a little. Six figures stand around it in the same soft mask. They whisper "milady" like Bosch missed the first half of a joke. They do not look up.
MiFella: I got my picture. I really do not want to be up here anymore. Let's go home and sleep.

cutscenes.comment: New opening return-home scene inside Bosch's house. MiFella walks to the interior exit and is hidden only after reaching the door.

pages: MiFella replays his photos of the crowd.
MiFella whispers "milady" once under his breath.
Everyone up there seemed so cool. Like they knew something we did not.

cutscenes.comment: New opening dawn transition at Bosch's bed. Setting both aliases keeps the existing Act 1 chain armed when the replacement gates are enabled.

pages: Bosch goes back to bed.

cutscenes.comment: MiFella waits outside Bosch's house, calls him toward the meteor, then runs down the south and west route and disappears ahead.

pages: MiFella: There you are! It came down on the hill, I watched it happen!
MiFella: Come on, follow me!

cutscenes.comment: STRAWBERRY prologue (Tier-1 keystone from the emotional-spine work). Warm birthday flashback of Bosch + MiFella BEFORE the photograph, staged with the music/camera/fx ops. Fires on prologue:active (set by the pre-wake boot wiring); prop actors 910220-910223 spawn on that flag. Ends by flashing white + cutting music to silence + fading to black, then setFlag prologue:done (despawns the props) so the boot wiring hands to the wake: Bosch wakes ALONE in the corrected present.

pages: Bosch's birthday. The cake made it out of the oven, mostly.
MiFella eats the fallen corner off the floor to make Bosch laugh. "Strawberry. You hate the frosting."
The family picture hangs a little crooked, the way it always does. You get up to fix it.
